name: Convergence of Linear Multistep Methods
statement_text:
Let [Оұ, ОІ] denote a preconsistent, stable linear multistep
method and let О· denote a solution of (422a). Suppose that yi is represented
by О· i for i = 0, 1, 2, . . . , k вҲ’ 1; then yi is represented by О· i for i = k, k + 1, . . . .
proof_text:
Proof. The proof is by induction, and it will only be necessary to show that
yk is represented by О· k , since this is a typical case. Multiply (422a) on the left
by О· k and we find that

  О· k вҲ’ Оұ1 О· kвҲ’1 вҲ’ Оұ2 О· kвҲ’2 вҲ’ В· В· В· вҲ’ Оұk
                              вҲ’ ОІ0 О· k D вҲ’ ОІ1 О· kвҲ’1 D вҲ’ ОІ2 О· kвҲ’2 D вҲ’ В· В· В· вҲ’ ОІk D = 0,
so that yk is represented by О· k .
proof_latex:
\begin{proof}
The proof is by induction, and it will only be necessary to show that $y_k$ is represented by $\eta^k$, since this is a typical case. Multiply \eqref{eq:422a} on the left by $\eta^k$ and we find that
\[
\eta^k - \alpha_1 \eta^{k-1} - \alpha_2 \eta^{k-2} - \cdots - \alpha_k - \beta_0 \eta^k D - \beta_1 \eta^{k-1} D - \beta_2 \eta^{k-2} D - \cdots - \beta_k D = 0,
\]
so that $y_k$ is represented by $\eta^k$.

The concept of an underlying one-step method was introduced by Kirchgraber (1986). Although the underlying method cannot be represented as a Runge--Kutta method, it can be represented as a B-series or, what is equivalent, in the manner that has been introduced here. Of more recent developments, the extension to general linear methods (Stoffer, 1993) is of particular interest. This generalization will be considered in Subsection 535.
\end{proof}

variables.name: [Оұ, ОІ]
variables.definition: a linear multistep method, with Оұ and ОІ being coefficient vectors

variables.name: О·
variables.definition: a solution to equation (422a), representing a mapping from trees to real numbers (a member of G1), interpreted as the underlying one-step method

variables.name: yi
variables.definition: the numerical solution at step i, represented by О·^i (the i-th power of О· in the group G1)

variables.name: k
variables.definition: the number of steps in the linear multistep method

equations.content: 1(u) вҲ’ Оұ1 О·^{вҲ’1}(u) вҲ’ Оұ2 О·^{вҲ’2}(u) вҲ’ В·В·В· вҲ’ Оұk О·^{вҲ’k}(u) вҲ’ ОІ0 D(u) вҲ’ ОІ1 О·^{вҲ’1} D(u) вҲ’ ОІ2 О·^{вҲ’2} D(u) вҲ’ В·В·В· вҲ’ ОІk О·^{вҲ’k} D(u) = 0, for all trees u

preamble: The context involves a preconsistent, stable linear multistep method [Оұ, ОІ] and a solution О· to a functional equation (422a) defined on trees, where О· is interpreted as the underlying one-step method in the group G1. The notation О·^i represents the i-th power of О· in this group, and yi is the numerical solution represented by О·^i.

dependencies.name: stability in the sense of Dahlquist
